cot_prefix: Okay, let me work through these instructions step by step. First, take 3 steps. Then take 7 more steps. So far, I've moved a total of 3 + 7 = 10 steps forward. Next, I'm told to turn around. Turning around would reverse my direction. So instead of moving forward, I'm now facing the opposite direction. Then, I take 10 steps in this new direction. Since I turned around, these 10 steps would take me back in the direction opposite to my initial movement. Let me summarize: - Start at point A - Move 3 steps forward to point B - Move 7 more steps forward to point C (total of 10 steps from point A) - Turn around at point C - Move 10 steps in the new direction to point D Since I took 10 steps forward initially and then 10 steps backward after turning around, I should end up back at my starting point A. Let me double-check: - First, I went 10 steps forward (3 + 7) - Then I turned around and went 10 steps back - 10
